human_reference_baseline.note: human-sent replies scored reference-free on the same rubric — a hard-data sanity anchor for the system score

per_response.reply: I'm really sorry to hear your watch has stopped working again—that's frustrating, especially after it happened before. We want to make this right for you. Could you tell me a bit more about what's happening with the watch (for example, won't turn on, display issue, etc.) and roughly when the first problem occurred? Once I understand the full picture, I'll get you sorted with a replacement or repair right away.

reasons.intent_resolution: The reply acknowledges the core frustration and commits to resolution, but stops short of immediately offering a concrete next step—it asks for more information first, which is reasonable but delays actual resolution.
reasons.factual_correctness: The candidate makes no specific factual claims (no order numbers, dates, or policies) and remains internally consistent with the incoming email's complaint.
reasons.faithfulness: The reply avoids inventing policies, refund amounts, or specific promises; it appropriately offers general remedies (replacement or repair) contingent on understanding the issue better.
reasons.completeness: The reply addresses the main complaint (watch not working a second time) and asks clarifying questions, but does not explicitly acknowledge the customer's frustration about the product quality or their comment about being 'stupid to buy'—a brief validation of that sentiment would have been more complete.
reasons.tone: The tone is empathetic, professional, and appropriately acknowledges frustration without being defensive; it validates the customer's experience and conveys genuine intent to help.

per_response.reply: I'm sorry you're still experiencing this issue even after reinstalling—that's frustrating. We've escalated this to our technical team to investigate what's causing the error in the app. In the meantime, you may be able to access your account through a web browser as a temporary workaround. Our team will work to get this resolved as quickly as possible, and we'll follow up with you as soon as we have an update.

reasons.intent_resolution: The reply acknowledges the customer's frustration and commits to escalation and follow-up, but does not ask clarifying questions about the specific error, which would be needed to fully resolve the issue.
reasons.factual_correctness: The reply makes no specific factual claims that could be incorrect; it offers general troubleshooting (web browser workaround) and escalation without inventing details.
reasons.faithfulness: The reply avoids inventing policies, timelines, or specific promises; it appropriately commits only to escalation and follow-up without overcommitting.
reasons.completeness: The reply addresses the core issue (app error after reinstall) but does not ask for the specific error message or details that would help the technical team, leaving a gap in information gathering.
reasons.tone: The tone is empathetic, professional, and appropriately acknowledges the customer's frustration while offering reassurance and a concrete next step.

per_response.reply: Thanks for reaching out. I understand how frustrating it is when useful information gets removed from an app—unfortunately, I don't have details about the specific changes made in version 11.1 or Apple's App Store display options on my end. Your best bet would be to contact our Aftersales team via Live Chat or phone at 0344 556 5650, and they can look into what happened with that feature and help you find the information you need.

reasons.intent_resolution: The reply acknowledges the customer's frustration but deflects to another team rather than attempting to answer the core question about how to see update sizes or explaining the removal.
reasons.factual_correctness: The candidate appropriately avoids inventing specific details about version 11.1 changes and correctly directs to a support channel, though the phone number should be verified for accuracy.
reasons.faithfulness: The reply avoids hallucinating specific policies or features, though it could have offered more general guidance about App Store functionality without claiming certainty.
reasons.completeness: The reply addresses only the routing aspect and does not attempt to answer either of the customer's two questions: how to see update sizes or why the information was removed.
reasons.tone: The tone is empathetic and professional, appropriately acknowledging the customer's frustration, though the deflection may feel dismissive.

per_response.reply: Hi there. I'd be happy to help you find that information. To give you the exact departure time for the train at Platform 14, could you let me know what date you're travelling? Once I have that, I can look up the specific service for you.

reasons.intent_resolution: The reply appropriately seeks the missing information (travel date) needed to answer the customer's question, though it does not yet provide the actual departure time.
reasons.factual_correctness: The candidate makes no factual claims that could be incorrect; it only asks a clarifying question.
reasons.faithfulness: The reply does not invent any policies, times, or promises—it simply requests necessary information to help.
reasons.completeness: The customer asked for a specific departure time, but the candidate does not provide it; instead it defers pending a date, leaving the core question unanswered.
reasons.tone: The tone is professional, helpful, and courteous, with appropriate empathy ('I'd be happy to help').

per_response.reply: I'm sorry to hear about the rough handling of your sister's package—that's frustrating, and I understand your concern about potential damage. Please let us know if the item arrived broken or if there are any issues with what was inside. If there is damage, we're happy to help make it right. You can reply here with details, and we'll get it sorted for you.

reasons.intent_resolution: The reply appropriately invites the customer to report damage and offers help, but stops short of proactively investigating the delivery behavior or committing to address the carrier conduct issue the customer witnessed.
reasons.factual_correctness: The reply makes no unsupported factual claims and remains internally consistent with the situation described in the incoming email.
reasons.faithfulness: The reply avoids inventing policies, refund amounts, or promises beyond what is reasonable (damage assessment and resolution if warranted).
reasons.completeness: The reply addresses the damage concern but does not acknowledge or respond to the customer's broader complaint about the delivery driver's rough handling behavior across multiple locations, which was a key part of their frustration.
reasons.tone: The tone is empathetic and professional, appropriately acknowledging frustration, though it could have been slightly more validating of the customer's concern about the carrier's conduct.

per_response.reply: Hi there! I'm sorry you're experiencing trouble connecting. That "Access Denied" error typically indicates a permissions issue with your account or session. A few quick things to try: refresh your browser, clear your cache, or try a different browser or device. If you're still seeing that error after those steps, please reply with your account email address and I can escalate this to our technical team to investigate further.

reasons.intent_resolution: The reply addresses the customer's error and offers troubleshooting steps plus escalation, but doesn't fully resolve the immediate panic or provide a clear next step timeline.
reasons.factual_correctness: The explanation of 'Access Denied' as a permissions issue is technically sound and the troubleshooting suggestions are standard and appropriate.
reasons.faithfulness: The reply avoids inventing specific policies, refund amounts, or unsupported promises; it sticks to general troubleshooting and a reasonable escalation path.
reasons.completeness: The reply addresses the error and offers solutions, but doesn't acknowledge the customer's stated panic or the specific reference ID they provided in the error message.
reasons.tone: The tone is professional and empathetic ('I'm sorry you're experiencing trouble'), though it could be slightly warmer given the customer's evident distress ('Panic!').

per_response.reply: I appreciate you reaching out, but it sounds like you may have contacted the wrong company—we're not Lyft. If you're having trouble with a Lyft ride, I'd recommend reaching out to Lyft's support team directly through their app or website so they can help resolve this with your driver right away.

reasons.intent_resolution: The reply correctly identifies the core issue (wrong company contacted) and provides the appropriate next step (contact Lyft directly), which resolves the customer's ability to get help.
reasons.factual_correctness: The reply is internally consistent and factually sound—it correctly identifies that this is a Lyft issue, not an issue for the support team receiving the email.
reasons.faithfulness: The reply makes no unsupported claims or invented policies; it simply clarifies the company boundary and directs the customer appropriately.
reasons.completeness: The incoming email contains one core complaint (driver doesn't know how to get to the house), and the reply addresses it by routing to the correct company that can actually help.
reasons.tone: The tone is professional, courteous, and empathetic—acknowledging the customer's frustration while helpfully redirecting them without dismissiveness.

per_response.reply: Thanks for reaching out! To permanently delete items from your recently played list, you can typically access this through your library settings or account preferences—the exact steps depend on which platform or service you're using. Could you let me know which app or service you're referring to so I can give you the most accurate guidance?

reasons.intent_resolution: The reply acknowledges the customer's question and offers to help, but defers providing the actual solution by asking for clarification rather than attempting to resolve the issue directly.
reasons.factual_correctness: The candidate correctly notes that deletion steps vary by platform and appropriately asks for clarification before providing specific instructions, which is factually sound.
reasons.faithfulness: The reply makes no unsupported claims or invented policies; it only offers general guidance and requests necessary information to provide accurate help.
reasons.completeness: While the reply addresses the core question, it does not provide any concrete steps or examples that might help the customer immediately, leaving the issue unresolved pending their response.
reasons.tone: The tone is friendly, professional, and helpful, with appropriate acknowledgment of the customer's question and a collaborative approach to finding the right solution.

per_response.reply:
Hi there, thanks for reaching out! Setting up a default page in your Azure WebApp is straightforward—you'll want to make sure your default document (like index.html) is named correctly and placed in the root directory of your site. Azure typically looks for files like index.html, default.html, or default.htm in order of priority.

A few things to check: First, verify that your HTML file is actually in the root folder (not in a subfolder) where you uploaded it via FTP. Second, check your WebApp's application settings to confirm the default document is configured—you can do this in the Azure portal under your WebApp's Configuration settings. If you've already set a default document there and it's still not working, let me know what error you're seeing or what happens when you visit your site, and I'll help troubleshoot further.

reasons.intent_resolution: The reply directly addresses the customer's question about how to set up a default page in Azure WebApp with clear, actionable steps.
reasons.factual_correctness: The technical guidance about default document naming (index.html, default.html, default.htm) and placement in the root directory is accurate for Azure WebApp configuration.
reasons.faithfulness: The reply provides general technical guidance without inventing specific policies, order numbers, or unsupported promises; all statements are consistent with standard Azure WebApp behavior.
reasons.completeness: The reply covers the customer's core question about setting up a default page and provides multiple troubleshooting angles (file naming, location, portal configuration, and offer for further help).
reasons.tone: The tone is professional, helpful, and empathetic—acknowledging the customer's issue and offering continued support if needed.

per_response.reply: Thanks for getting in touch, and I'm sorry you're having a frustrating experience. I'd like to help track down what's going on and get it sorted for you. Could you give me a bit more detail about what problems you're running into? Once I understand what's happening, I'll do my best to find a solution.

reasons.intent_resolution: The reply appropriately seeks clarification but cannot resolve the customer's complaint without understanding the specific problems, leaving the core issue unaddressed.
reasons.factual_correctness: The reply makes no specific factual claims and contains no internal contradictions or unsupported assertions.
reasons.faithfulness: The reply avoids inventing policies, prices, or promises and stays within appropriate bounds of what a support agent can offer.
reasons.completeness: The incoming email is vague and lacks detail, but the candidate does not acknowledge the customer's frustration or validate their experience before asking for more information, missing an opportunity to show empathy upfront.
reasons.tone: The tone is professional and empathetic, appropriately acknowledging frustration, though it could be slightly warmer given the customer's evident irritation.

per_response.reply: Hi there! Thank you for reaching out. We understand your concern about unexpected services on your lines. To help you deactivate any active data bundles or services on 08051148842 and 09052236815, we'll need to look into your account details. Please reply with any additional information you can provide, and we'll get this sorted for you right away.

reasons.intent_resolution: The reply acknowledges the customer's request to deactivate services but does not commit to or complete the deactivation—it only asks for more information, leaving the core request unresolved.
reasons.factual_correctness: The reply correctly identifies the two phone numbers provided and accurately describes the customer's concern without stating any unsupported facts.
reasons.faithfulness: The reply makes no invented promises, policies, or specific commitments beyond offering to investigate and help, which is appropriate given the request.
reasons.completeness: The reply addresses the deactivation request and acknowledges both phone numbers, but does not explain what specific information is needed or provide a clear next step for resolution.
reasons.tone: The tone is professional and empathetic, appropriately acknowledging the customer's concern, though it could be slightly more reassuring given the customer's request for prompt action.

per_response.reply:
I'm really sorry to hear about this distressing experience—that's absolutely not acceptable, especially for a passenger with medical needs. We take these concerns very seriously.

To help us investigate and make this right, could you please share your booking reference or ticket number, along with the train route and date of travel? Once I have those details, I can look into what happened with the facilities and connect you with the right team to address this properly.

reasons.intent_resolution: The reply acknowledges the serious concern and commits to investigating, but stops short of offering concrete resolution (e.g., compensation, formal complaint process) without first gathering details.
reasons.factual_correctness: The reply makes no specific factual claims that could be incorrect; it appropriately requests information before committing to facts.
reasons.faithfulness: The reply avoids inventing policies, compensation amounts, or specific remedies, instead asking for details needed to proceed appropriately.
reasons.completeness: The reply addresses the core complaint but does not acknowledge or respond to the specific mention of the passenger being diabetic and distressed, which warrants explicit recognition of the medical/accessibility angle.
reasons.tone: The tone is empathetic, takes the complaint seriously, and appropriately validates the customer's distress while remaining professional.

per_response.reply: We understand your concern about the tight delivery window. To help you best, could you please share your order number and the address where the package should be delivered? In the meantime, if you're worried about missing the delivery, you can try to be available during the estimated window or let us know if there's a specific issue preventing delivery to your door—we'll work with our delivery team to find a solution.

reasons.intent_resolution: The reply acknowledges the customer's concern but does not directly resolve the urgent delivery issue; instead it asks for information and offers vague next steps rather than concrete action or reassurance.
reasons.factual_correctness: The reply makes no specific factual claims that could be incorrect; it stays general and does not contradict any facts in the incoming email.
reasons.faithfulness: The reply avoids inventing policies or promises, though the suggestion to 'work with our delivery team to find a solution' is somewhat vague and could be more specific about what is actually possible.
reasons.completeness: The incoming email is fragmented and unclear, but the core issue is an urgent delivery problem with only 2.5 hours remaining; the candidate does not address whether the delivery can still be made or offer concrete alternatives like rescheduling or pickup options.
reasons.tone: The tone is professional and empathetic, appropriately acknowledging the customer's frustration, though it could be more urgent and action-oriented given the time-sensitive nature of the complaint.

per_response.reply: Hi! Thanks for reaching out. To help you track down your 1-hour delivery slot, could you share your order number? Once I have that, I can look up the latest status and let you know when you should expect the notification with your specific time window.

reasons.factual_correctness: The reply makes no unsupported factual claims and correctly understands the customer's situation (flexi delivery with a 4-hour window, awaiting a 1-hour slot notification).
reasons.faithfulness: The reply avoids inventing policies, timelines, or promises; it simply offers to look up the status once given the order number, which is a reasonable and supportable commitment.
reasons.completeness: The reply addresses the core question about when the customer will receive their slot notification, but doesn't provide any general guidance on typical timeframes or next steps if the notification is delayed.
reasons.tone: The tone is friendly, professional, and appropriately helpful—using 'Thanks for reaching out' and offering concrete assistance without being dismissive of the customer's concern.

per_response.reply: Morning! Thanks for reaching out. We appreciate your interest in supporting charity events. reasons.intent_resolution: The reply acknowledges the customer's question about charity event support and commits to exploring the possibility, but stops short of providing a definitive answer or clear next steps for resolution.
reasons.factual_correctness: The candidate makes no specific factual claims that could be incorrect; it appropriately avoids inventing policies or commitments without knowledge.
reasons.faithfulness: The reply does not invent any policies, donation amounts, or specific programs; it honestly states lack of immediate details and offers to connect with the appropriate team.
reasons.completeness: The reply addresses the core question about charity event support but could have been more complete by asking about the specific type of tickets or support needed (e.g., quantity, event size, timeline).

per_response.reply: I understand how frustrating it is to be without your OnePlus 5T for over a week—I'm sorry you're experiencing this delay. To help get this resolved quickly, could you share your order number and let me know what specifically is holding up delivery (for example, is it stuck in transit, pending payment, or something else)? Once I have those details, I'll be able to look into what's happening and work toward getting this sorted for you right away.

reasons.intent_resolution: The reply acknowledges the frustration and promises to help, but does not actually resolve the issue or commit to a specific remedy—it only requests information to begin investigating.
reasons.factual_correctness: The candidate makes no specific factual claims about policies, dates, or amounts, and remains internally consistent with the incoming email's context.

per_response.reply:
Hi there,

Thanks for reaching out about the printhead issue with your HP Officejet Pro 8100. I'd like to help get this resolved for you. To best assist, could you let me know a bit more about what you're experiencing—is the printhead physically missing, or are you seeing error messages indicating a failure? Also, have you already tried any troubleshooting steps like cleaning the printhead or running a diagnostic?

In the meantime, you can also reach out to our support team directly through a private message with your serial number and we can explore replacement or repair options for you.

Thanks,
[Support Team]

reasons.intent_resolution: The reply acknowledges the printhead issue and offers to help, but stops short of committing to a concrete resolution path—it asks clarifying questions rather than moving toward a definitive next step like replacement or repair.
reasons.factual_correctness: The candidate makes no specific factual claims about policies, pricing, or procedures that could be incorrect; all statements are appropriately general and internally consistent.
reasons.faithfulness: The reply avoids inventing specific policies, refund amounts, or guarantees; it only offers general troubleshooting guidance and mentions exploring 'replacement or repair options' without overpromising.
reasons.completeness: The incoming email is sparse and provides no specific details about the nature of the problem, so the candidate reasonably asks clarifying questions, but does not fully commit to a resolution path or explain what the next concrete steps will be after information is provided.
reasons.tone: The tone is professional, empathetic, and helpful—acknowledging the customer's issue, offering assistance, and inviting further communication in a friendly manner.

per_response.reply: We're really sorry to hear about the delay—that's frustrating, especially when you chose what seemed like the best option. While we can't control mechanical issues with the aircraft, we'd like to make this right. Could you share your booking confirmation number or the phone number on your account so we can look into what happened and discuss how we can help?

reasons.intent_resolution: The reply acknowledges the delay complaint and offers to investigate and help, but stops short of committing to a specific resolution (refund, credit, rebooking) that the customer's frustrated tone suggests they may be seeking.
reasons.factual_correctness: The reply makes no unsupported factual claims and correctly acknowledges that mechanical aircraft issues are typically outside the airline's direct control.
reasons.faithfulness: The reply avoids inventing policies, refund amounts, or specific remedies; it appropriately asks for information before committing to a course of action.
reasons.completeness: The reply addresses the core complaint (3-hour delay due to plane issues) and requests booking details, but does not explicitly acknowledge or respond to the customer's implicit concern about the value proposition ('cheapest ticket' vs. poor outcome).
reasons.tone: The tone is empathetic, acknowledges frustration, and maintains professionalism while showing willingness to help—appropriate for a service failure complaint.

per_response.reply: Hi there, I'm sorry you're running into trouble completing your upgrade order—that's frustrating. I can see you've attached an image of the issue, which is really helpful. This sounds like it could be a browser or payment processing glitch. A few quick things to try: clear your browser cache and cookies, try a different browser if you have one handy, or attempt the order on a mobile device if you were using a computer (or vice versa). If you're still stuck after that, please reach out to our support team directly and they'll be able to walk through this with you in real time and get your upgrade sorted.

reasons.intent_resolution: The reply addresses the customer's core issue (stuck payment page) and offers practical troubleshooting steps, but stops short of committing to a direct resolution path or escalation timeline.
reasons.factual_correctness: The candidate makes no specific factual claims about policies, dates, or amounts; all suggestions (clearing cache, trying different browsers/devices) are standard and internally consistent.
reasons.faithfulness: The reply avoids inventing policies or promises; it offers general troubleshooting guidance and appropriately defers to the support team for hands-on help without overcommitting.
reasons.completeness: The reply covers the main issue and offers multiple troubleshooting paths, but does not explicitly acknowledge or address the attached image beyond a brief mention, nor does it confirm receipt of the image or offer to review it directly.
reasons.tone: The tone is empathetic, professional, and appropriately reassuring for a frustrated customer experiencing a technical issue.

per_response.reply: I hear your frustration—crew logistics issues that delay flights are genuinely aggravating, especially when the plane is ready to go. While we appreciate you acknowledging our response, I understand that doesn't solve the core problem of getting you where you need to be on time. Since this involves SkyWest operations, I'd recommend reaching out to them directly about scheduling and staffing concerns, as those decisions fall outside our control. That said, if there's anything we can help with on our end regarding your booking or experience, please let me know.

reasons.intent_resolution: The reply acknowledges the customer's frustration and offers a path forward (contacting SkyWest directly), but stops short of proposing concrete next steps or compensation review that might fully resolve the underlying service failure.
reasons.factual_correctness: The candidate correctly identifies that crew logistics fall under SkyWest's purview and appropriately defers to them without making unsupported claims about policies or procedures.
reasons.faithfulness: The reply avoids inventing specific policies, refund amounts, or promises; it stays grounded in what the sender can reasonably offer (help with booking/experience) and what they cannot control (SkyWest staffing).
reasons.completeness: The reply addresses the core complaint about crew logistics and the frustration, but does not explicitly ask clarifying questions about whether the customer missed their flight, needs rebooking, or seeks compensation—details that would help fully resolve the issue.
reasons.tone: The tone is empathetic, validating the customer's frustration while remaining professional and constructive; it acknowledges the backhanded compliment gracefully without becoming defensive.

reasons.factual_correctness: The statement that Adobe regularly releases updates for Lightroom Classic CC is accurate and consistent with industry practice, though the candidate does not confirm or deny a specific update today.
reasons.faithfulness: The reply makes only general, supportable claims about regular updates and does not invent specific dates, version numbers, features, or policies.

per_response.reply: Thanks for reaching out about this. I understand your privacy concerns. per_response.reply:
Thanks for reaching out! I understand how frustrating that must be—it's great that your AppleTV is working well on the wired connection, but I can see why you'd want to use your phone as a remote too. 

The issue you're running into is that Apple's Remote app requires your phone and AppleTV to be on the same network (WiFi or wired). Since your AppleTV is wired and your phone connects via WiFi, they're technically on different networks, which is why the remote can't discover it.

Here are a couple of options: you can connect your AppleTV to WiFi instead of wired (if that works for your setup), or if you need to keep it wired, you could try connecting your phone to the same WiFi network your AppleTV uses—many routers bridge wired and wireless devices on the same network, so this might actually work. If you'd like to troubleshoot further, let me know your router model and I can give you more specific guidance.

reasons.intent_resolution: The candidate addresses the core problem and offers two viable solutions, but doesn't fully resolve the issue—it leaves the customer to try the suggestions without confirming whether they'll work or offering a definitive path forward.
reasons.factual_correctness: The explanation that the Remote app requires devices on the same network is reasonable and internally consistent, though the claim that wired and WiFi are 'technically on different networks' is slightly imprecise—most home routers bridge them, which the candidate acknowledges later.
reasons.faithfulness: The candidate avoids inventing specific policies, product features, or unsupported claims; all statements are general troubleshooting guidance consistent with typical Apple Remote behavior.
reasons.completeness: The candidate addresses the main complaint and offers two workarounds, but doesn't explore other potential solutions (e.g., checking network settings, ensuring both devices are on the same subnet, or suggesting Apple Support if the issue persists).
reasons.tone: The tone is empathetic, acknowledges the customer's frustration, validates their working setup, and maintains a helpful, professional demeanor throughout.

per_response.reply: I'm sorry you're experiencing these issues with your iPhone 7 Plus—that's definitely frustrating. We'd like to help get this sorted out for you. To better understand what's happening, could you let me know which app or apps are glitching and freezing? Also, when did you install the update, and have you tried restarting your phone or reinstalling the affected app? In the meantime, we can look into whether there's a known issue on our end that might be causing the slower charging.

reasons.intent_resolution: The reply acknowledges the customer's frustration and offers to help, but stops short of committing to a concrete resolution path—it asks diagnostic questions rather than proposing next steps like a replacement, rollback, or escalation.
reasons.factual_correctness: The reply makes no unsupported factual claims and correctly identifies the device model (iPhone 7 Plus) and the general nature of the reported issues.
reasons.faithfulness: The reply avoids inventing policies, refund amounts, or specific remedies; it only asks clarifying questions and offers to investigate, which is appropriate given the limited information.
reasons.completeness: The reply addresses the glitching, freezing, and charging issues, but does not acknowledge or respond to the customer's threat to switch to Samsung—a signal of urgency and dissatisfaction that warrants at least a retention-focused acknowledgment.
reasons.tone: The tone is empathetic, professional, and appropriately reassuring; it validates the customer's frustration while maintaining a helpful, collaborative stance.

per_response.reply:
I'm sorry to hear your account may have been compromised—that's definitely concerning. Unfortunately, resetting an Apple ID is handled directly by Apple rather than our team, so you'll want to go to iforgot.apple.com or contact Apple Support directly to regain access to your account and update the phone number on file.

In the meantime, if you have any issues with our service or your account with us, please let me know and I'm happy to help sort that out.

reasons.intent_resolution: The reply correctly identifies that Apple ID resets are outside the support team's scope and directs the customer to the appropriate resource (iforgot.apple.com and Apple Support), which addresses the core intent, though it could have been slightly more proactive in offering interim account security steps.
reasons.factual_correctness: The response accurately states that Apple ID resets are handled by Apple directly and correctly points to iforgot.apple.com as the official Apple resource, with no factual errors or unsupported claims.
reasons.faithfulness: The reply makes no unsupported promises, invented policies, or claims beyond what is reasonable—it appropriately acknowledges the scope limitation and offers to help with company-specific issues if applicable.
reasons.completeness: The reply addresses the main question (how to reset Apple ID) and offers follow-up support for company-related issues, but does not provide additional security guidance (e.g., checking for unauthorized purchases, enabling two-factor authentication once access is regained) that would be helpful for a hacked account.
reasons.tone: The tone is empathetic, acknowledges the customer's concern about a potential compromise, and maintains professionalism while clearly explaining the limitation and next steps.

per_response.reply: Great question! The steps depend on which platform you're using. On PlayStation, you can check your purchase and transaction history by going to Settings > Account Management > Account Information > Wallet > Transaction History. For Xbox, you'll want to visit your account settings and look for "Purchase history" or "Order history." On iPhone, you can view your App Store purchase history by opening the App Store app, tapping your profile icon in the top right, and selecting "Purchase history." Let me know if you need help navigating any of these or if you're looking for something more specific!

reasons.intent_resolution: The reply addresses how to view gaming history across multiple platforms, but the incoming email is somewhat ambiguous (unclear grammar: 'amp via Iphone') and the candidate doesn't clarify what the customer actually means or ask for specifics before providing detailed instructions.
reasons.factual_correctness: The instructions provided appear internally consistent and generally accurate for major platforms, though without a reference reply or access to current UI, minor details about menu paths could have shifted.
reasons.faithfulness: The reply provides only general, well-known platform navigation steps without inventing specific policies, prices, or unsupported promises.
reasons.completeness: The reply covers PlayStation, Xbox, and iPhone App Store, but the incoming email mentions 'gaming consoles amp via Iphone' which is grammatically unclear—the candidate doesn't ask for clarification about whether the customer wants history for specific consoles, game libraries, or account activity, leaving ambiguity unresolved.
reasons.tone: The tone is friendly, helpful, and professional, with an open invitation for follow-up questions.

per_response.reply: Hi there, thanks for reaching out! On our long-haul economy flights, we do have USB charging ports available at most seats, which should cover phones and smaller devices. For larger devices or if you need a standard power outlet, availability can vary depending on the aircraft type and specific route. If you let me know which flight you're booked on, I can confirm the exact charging setup for your journey.

reasons.intent_resolution: The reply directly addresses the customer's question about charging facilities on long-haul economy flights and provides actionable information.
reasons.factual_correctness: The response is internally consistent and plausible, though without a reference reply or company documentation, the specific claim about USB ports at most seats cannot be verified; the caveat about aircraft variation is appropriately cautious.
reasons.faithfulness: The reply avoids inventing specific policies or guarantees, appropriately hedges with 'most seats' and 'can vary,' and offers to confirm details for the specific flight rather than making blanket promises.
reasons.completeness: The reply fully addresses the customer's single question about charging facilities, covers both USB and standard outlets, acknowledges variability, and offers a next step to provide personalized information.
reasons.tone: The tone is friendly, professional, and helpful—opening with thanks, using conversational language ('Hey there'), and offering personalized assistance.

per_response.reply:
I understand how stressful that is—I'm sorry you're dealing with a stolen card. However, I'm not able to shut off or freeze your credit card directly, as that needs to go through your bank or credit card issuer. I'd recommend calling them right away to report the theft and request an immediate freeze or cancellation.

If you have any charges on your account with us that you'd like us to look into or dispute, please let me know your booking details and I'm happy to help with that side of things.

reasons.intent_resolution: The reply correctly identifies that the candidate cannot directly shut off the card and appropriately redirects to the bank, but could have been more proactive in offering to help with any fraudulent charges on the company's own platform.
reasons.factual_correctness: The reply accurately states that credit card freezing/cancellation must be handled by the bank or issuer, which is factually correct and internally consistent.
reasons.faithfulness: The reply makes no unsupported promises or invented policies; it honestly explains the company's limitations and offers legitimate help with account-related charges.
reasons.completeness: The reply addresses the main request and offers follow-up support for any fraudulent charges on the company's account, though it could have asked whether the customer has already contacted their bank or needs additional guidance.
reasons.tone: The tone is empathetic, acknowledging the customer's stress while remaining professional and helpful throughout.
